title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery and Temple discuss the motives of the Reinke couple, suggesting they targeted Miss Bradley because of her status as a millionaire's daughter. Temple reveals that he had been searching for a hidden treasure for two weeks. This morning, while digging at a specific location, he was knocked unconscious by Reinke (also known as Tracdon), who stole the loot and fled with his wife. The group discovers a one-foot-square hole in the woods near the house.

Despite Inspector Vane’s efforts to arrest the couple, they remain at large. Vane sends telegrams to several European cities. On Saturday, three responses arrive: Paris identifies a couple matching the description as Percy Strong and his wife, wanted for robbery in 1925; Budapest identifies them as Traklund and his wife, wanted for jewelry theft since 1920; and Vienna identifies them as Percy and Beth Anlexter, wanted for defrauding a tourist and stealing jewelry. The authorities request the extradition of stolen goods. An officer notes that the fugitives' crimes have created an international dispute between France, Hungary, and Austria.

A telegram from Belgrade, Yugoslavia, explains a delay in reporting on the Twerly brothers and Vilga Krosak due to the dissolution of Montenegro and ongoing investigations into a blood feud. Meanwhile, Ellery and Professor Yadley discuss the lack of progress regarding the murder and the mysterious figure Crosetto. Later, Stephen Megalas arrives at a colonial-era mansion, confronting Inspector Vawn, Ellery, the professor, Helen Bred, Jonathan Lincoln, District Attorney Easam, and Captain Swift. Megalas demands accountability for the three weeks following Bred's death.
